result.stdout:
Description:
  Generate Managed<->IL2CPP interop assemblies from Cpp2IL's output.

Usage:
  Il2CppInterop.CLI [command] [options]

Options:
  --verbose       Produce more verbose output
  --version       Show version information
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  deobf        Tools for deobfuscating assemblies
  generate     Generate wrapper assemblies that can be used to interop with Il2Cpp
  wrapper-gen  Tools for generating Il2Cpp struct wrappers from libi2lcpp source

commands.payload:
Description:
  Generate Managed<->IL2CPP interop assemblies from Cpp2IL's output.

Usage:
  Il2CppInterop.CLI [command] [options]

Options:
  --verbose       Produce more verbose output
  --version       Show version information
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  deobf        Tools for deobfuscating assemblies
  generate     Generate wrapper assemblies that can be used to interop with Il2Cpp
  wrapper-gen  Tools for generating Il2Cpp struct wrappers from libi2lcpp source

result.stdout:
Description:
  Tools for deobfuscating assemblies

Usage:
  Il2CppInterop.CLI deobf [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  analyze   Analyze deobfuscation performance with different parameter values. Will not generate assemblies.
  generate  Generate a deobfuscation map from original unobfuscated assemblies. Will not generate assemblies.

commands.payload:
Description:
  Tools for deobfuscating assemblies

Usage:
  Il2CppInterop.CLI deobf [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  analyze   Analyze deobfuscation performance with different parameter values. Will not generate assemblies.
  generate  Generate a deobfuscation map from original unobfuscated assemblies. Will not generate assemblies.

result.stdout:
Description:
  Analyze deobfuscation performance with different parameter values. Will not generate assemblies.

Usage:
  Il2CppInterop.CLI deobf analyze [options]

Options:
  --input <input> (REQUIRED)                 Directory of assemblies to deobfuscate
  --add-prefix-to <add-prefix-to>            Assemblies and namespaces starting with these will get an Il2Cpp prefix in generated assemblies. Allows multiple values. Obsolete.
  --dont-add-prefix-to <dont-add-prefix-to>  Assemblies and namespaces starting with these will not get an Il2Cpp prefix in generated assemblies. Allows multiple values.
  --use-opt-out-prefixing                    Assemblies and namespaces will get an Il2Cpp prefix in generated assemblies unless otherwise specified. Obsolete.
  -?, -h, --help                             Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Analyze deobfuscation performance with different parameter values. Will not generate assemblies.

Usage:
  Il2CppInterop.CLI deobf analyze [options]

Options:
  --input <input> (REQUIRED)                 Directory of assemblies to deobfuscate
  --add-prefix-to <add-prefix-to>            Assemblies and namespaces starting with these will get an Il2Cpp prefix in generated assemblies. Allows multiple values. Obsolete.
  --dont-add-prefix-to <dont-add-prefix-to>  Assemblies and namespaces starting with these will not get an Il2Cpp prefix in generated assemblies. Allows multiple values.
  --use-opt-out-prefixing                    Assemblies and namespaces will get an Il2Cpp prefix in generated assemblies unless otherwise specified. Obsolete.
  -?, -h, --help                             Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Generate a deobfuscation map from original unobfuscated assemblies. Will not generate assemblies.

Usage:
  Il2CppInterop.CLI deobf generate [options]

Options:
  --old-assemblies <old-assemblies> (REQUIRED)  Directory with old unobfuscated assemblies
  --new-assemblies <new-assemblies> (REQUIRED)  Directory to write obfuscation maps to
  --output <output> (REQUIRED)                  Directory to write obfuscation maps to
  --include <include>                           Include these assemblies for deobfuscation map generation. If none are specified, all assemblies will be included.
  --deobf-uniq-chars <deobf-uniq-chars>         How many characters per unique token to use during deobfuscation
  --deobf-uniq-max <deobf-uniq-max>             How many maximum unique tokens per type are allowed during deobfuscation
  --obf-regex <obf-regex>                       Specifies a regex for obfuscated names. All types and members matching will be renamed.
  -?, -h, --help                                Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Generate a deobfuscation map from original unobfuscated assemblies. Will not generate assemblies.

Usage:
  Il2CppInterop.CLI deobf generate [options]

Options:
  --old-assemblies <old-assemblies> (REQUIRED)  Directory with old unobfuscated assemblies
  --new-assemblies <new-assemblies> (REQUIRED)  Directory to write obfuscation maps to
  --output <output> (REQUIRED)                  Directory to write obfuscation maps to
  --include <include>                           Include these assemblies for deobfuscation map generation. If none are specified, all assemblies will be included.
  --deobf-uniq-chars <deobf-uniq-chars>         How many characters per unique token to use during deobfuscation
  --deobf-uniq-max <deobf-uniq-max>             How many maximum unique tokens per type are allowed during deobfuscation
  --obf-regex <obf-regex>                       Specifies a regex for obfuscated names. All types and members matching will be renamed.
  -?, -h, --help                                Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Generate wrapper assemblies that can be used to interop with Il2Cpp

Usage:
  Il2CppInterop.CLI generate [options]

Options:
  --input <input> (REQUIRED)                 Directory with Il2CppDumper's dummy assemblies
  --output <output> (REQUIRED)               Directory to write generated assemblies to
  --unity <unity>                            Directory with original Unity assemblies for unstripping
  --game-assembly <game-assembly>            Path to GameAssembly.dll. Used for certain analyses
  --no-xref-cache                            Don't generate xref scanning cache. All scanning will be done at runtime.
  --add-prefix-to <add-prefix-to>            Assemblies and namespaces starting with these will get an Il2Cpp prefix in generated assemblies. Allows multiple values. Obsolete.
  --dont-add-prefix-to <dont-add-prefix-to>  Assemblies and namespaces starting with these will not get an Il2Cpp prefix in generated assemblies. Allows multiple values.
  --use-opt-out-prefixing                    Assemblies and namespaces will get an Il2Cpp prefix in generated assemblies unless otherwise specified. Obsolete.
  --deobf-map <deobf-map>                    Specifies a file specifying deobfuscation map for obfuscated types and members.
  --deobf-uniq-chars <deobf-uniq-chars>      How many characters per unique token to use during deobfuscation
  --deobf-uniq-max <deobf-uniq-max>          How many maximum unique tokens per type are allowed during deobfuscation
  --blacklist-assembly <blacklist-assembly>  Don't write specified assembly to output. Allows multiple values.
  --obf-regex <obf-regex>                    Specifies a regex for obfuscated names. All types and members matching will be renamed.
  --passthrough-names                        If specified, names will be copied from input assemblies as-is without renaming or deobfuscation.
  --no-parallel                              Disable parallel processing when writing assemblies. Use if you encounter stability issues when generating assemblies.
  -?, -h, --help                             Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Generate wrapper assemblies that can be used to interop with Il2Cpp

Usage:
  Il2CppInterop.CLI generate [options]

Options:
  --input <input> (REQUIRED)                 Directory with Il2CppDumper's dummy assemblies
  --output <output> (REQUIRED)               Directory to write generated assemblies to
  --unity <unity>                            Directory with original Unity assemblies for unstripping
  --game-assembly <game-assembly>            Path to GameAssembly.dll. Used for certain analyses
  --no-xref-cache                            Don't generate xref scanning cache. All scanning will be done at runtime.
  --add-prefix-to <add-prefix-to>            Assemblies and namespaces starting with these will get an Il2Cpp prefix in generated assemblies. Allows multiple values. Obsolete.
  --dont-add-prefix-to <dont-add-prefix-to>  Assemblies and namespaces starting with these will not get an Il2Cpp prefix in generated assemblies. Allows multiple values.
  --use-opt-out-prefixing                    Assemblies and namespaces will get an Il2Cpp prefix in generated assemblies unless otherwise specified. Obsolete.
  --deobf-map <deobf-map>                    Specifies a file specifying deobfuscation map for obfuscated types and members.
  --deobf-uniq-chars <deobf-uniq-chars>      How many characters per unique token to use during deobfuscation
  --deobf-uniq-max <deobf-uniq-max>          How many maximum unique tokens per type are allowed during deobfuscation
  --blacklist-assembly <blacklist-assembly>  Don't write specified assembly to output. Allows multiple values.
  --obf-regex <obf-regex>                    Specifies a regex for obfuscated names. All types and members matching will be renamed.
  --passthrough-names                        If specified, names will be copied from input assemblies as-is without renaming or deobfuscation.
  --no-parallel                              Disable parallel processing when writing assemblies. Use if you encounter stability issues when generating assemblies.
  -?, -h, --help                             Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Tools for generating Il2Cpp struct wrappers from libi2lcpp source

Usage:
  Il2CppInterop.CLI wrapper-gen [options]

Options:
  --headers <headers> (REQUIRED)  Directory that contains libil2cpp headers. Directory must contains subdirectories named after libil2cpp version.
  --output <output> (REQUIRED)    Directory to write managed struct wrapper sources to
  -?, -h, --help                  Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:

Usage:

Options:
  --headers <headers> (REQUIRED)  Directory that contains libil2cpp headers. Directory must contains subdirectories named after libil2cpp version.
  --output <output> (REQUIRED)    Directory to write managed struct wrapper sources to
  -?, -h, --help                  Show help and usage information
